Using streamlined SAP Fiori apps and a human-centered approach of design thinking, agile delivery, and change management, it achieved faster troubleshooting and greater productivity, and set the stage for enterprise-wide innovation.
Wednesday, May 21. 11:30 AM - 12:10 PM EDT

PUX2821 | SAP's user-experience strategy to achieve better outcomes
Understand how SAP is leading the way to help business users get work done easily and with better outcomes. Our strategy provides a consistent and integrated suite-first experience, infuses AI nearly everywhere, makes it easier for our customers and partners to design and build great UX, and leverages our user research framework to meet your needs.
Arin Bhowmick, Chief Design Officer, SAP
Emil Voutta, Head of SAP Mobile Design System, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 3:30 PM - 3:50 PM EDT

PUX2824 | AI-first user experience: What's in it for you?
Discover what it means for users to have an AI-first experience. Examine how our AI design patterns and best practices can help you achieve better outcomes, both in context within your applications and as conversational experiences with the Joule copilot. See some highlights already available, and get a glimpse of where we are heading.
Arin Bhowmick, Chief Design Officer, SAP
Jennifer Aue, Chief AI Design Expert, SAP
Chris Allen, SAP
Wednesday, May 21. 11:00 AM - 11:20 AM EDT

PUX2825 | Suite-first experience: Seamless, consistent, and integrated
A suite-first approach elevates product and user experience by delivering consistency, integration, and efficiency across SAP solutions. Learn how the SAP Design System and central services create a seamless experience for web and mobile use cases, enhancing usability while helping ensure a cohesive and scalable product ecosystem for your business.
Arin Bhowmick, Chief Design Officer, SAP
Florian Heretsch, SVP, Head of Cross Engineering & Experience, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 1:00 PM - 1:20 PM EDT

PUX2826 | User experience innovations in our products: For today and tomorrow
Have a look at the latest UX innovations across our suite of applications, along with cool innovations coming soon. Examine SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA, as well as cloud products such as SAP SuccessFactors and SAP Ariba solutions. Also, check out selected highlights from UI technologies.
Tom Reiss, VP, SAP Fiori Product Management, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 4:30 PM - 4:50 PM EDT

PUX2827 | Creating a great UX for your own applications with SAP Design System
More easily create applications that look like those from SAP by using a powerful set of reusable components, patterns, and floor plans with the SAP Design System (SAP Fiori UX for web and for mobile), along with design guidelines on how to use them. Building applications is made easier through our AI-powered UI technologies for web and mobile apps.
Sophia Levens, Head of SAP Design System, SAP
Emil Voutta, Head of SAP Mobile Design System, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 1:30 PM - 1:50 PM EDT

 

Moving to SAP S/4HANA

Since we are delivering most of our AI innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, we want to make it as easy as possible for you to get there. For new customers, it’s easy; use SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for a ready-to-run cloud ERP system. For existing customers, there are different ways to upgrade, most of which involve moving to a clean core and using SAP Business Technology Platform. These sessions will help you plan and execute your move.

ASUG102 | Planning Your SAP S/4HANA Journey: From Cloud Choices To Change Management
Navigate SAP S/4HANA transformations in this full-day seminar. In our morning session, hear from SAP and customers on approaches, methodologies, and success stories. Then, choose your path to dive deeper: RISE and GROW with SAP applications or change management practices and tools to make a successful digital transformation.
Monday, May 19. 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM EDT

ERP2384 | Paths from SAP ECC to RISE with SAP: brownfield, greenfield, or bluefield
Customers looking to move to cloud ERP need the right path for their organization. What are the pitfalls? What are the advantages? Hear SAP experts, along with a panel of customers, conduct a Q&A on key considerations. Learn how your choice impacts your cloud ERP journey, cloud mindset, future adoption, and total cost of ownership.
Joseph Jones, Solution Advisor, S/4HANA Center of Excellence, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 3:00 PM - 3:40 PM EDT

ERP1672 | Mastering clean core for success with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Customer panelists share their awareness, understanding, and adoption of SAP’s clean core approach. Explore how it helps address technical debt and what organizations need to support this change. Learn about SAP’s clean core recommendations for migration to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and join an open discussion to share insights.
Peter Keller, Chief Sovereign Cloud Architect, SAP
Wednesday, May 21. 11:30 AM - 12:10 PM EDT

SER2113 | Unlock the business value of clean core during your RISE with SAP journey
Join SAP experts and pioneering reference customers for a deeper dive on the clean core approach. You can learn more about the business value framework involved in a clean core strategy. Discover the tools that are available for your organization and how to best leverage them during your business transformation.

BTP2074 | Maximize your move to SAP cloud ERP and SAP Business Suite with SAP BTP
Accelerate your migration to SAP cloud ERP solutions and SAP Business Suite with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). With tools to migrate data and custom reports, and integration to connect applications, SAP BTP offers generative AI development to keep a clean ERP core, and a foundation to build, deploy, and manage AI-powered solutions.
Terry Penner, Product Expert, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 3:00 PM - 3:20 PM EDT

ERP1667 | RISE with SAP in action: Stories of transformation, benefits, and lessons
Hear how diverse organizations use the RISE with SAP solution to drive digital transformation, modernize cloud architecture, and unlock value. Panelists share candid lessons learned, benefits achieved, and challenges faced. Whether you’re considering or optimizing RISE with SAP, gain insights and actionable takeaways to maximize your deployment.
Kristen Scheffler, VP Customer Engagement North America, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 11:00 AM - 11:40 AM EDT

 

Personal development and organizational change management

With the pace of technological change moving so fast, it’s critical to stay informed about new developments and how to apply them. And to help your colleagues adopt new innovations to transform your business. These sessions will equip you with the knowledge and tools you need to be a change agent in your organization.

DIS1669 | Future-proof your career: Embrace lifelong learning at SAP
In today’s digital landscape, SAP professionals must stay ahead by upgrading their skills. Explore the importance of lifelong learning to remain relevant in the SAP ecosystem. You can discover strategies to adapt to rapid tech changes, leverage learning resources from SAP, and foster a culture of continuous education for long-term success.
Kristen Scheffler, VP Customer Engagement North America, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 11:00 AM - 11:40 AM EDT

TRE1605 | Why business transformation is a struggle—and how to fix it
Business transformation is rarely smooth. In fact, a recent SAP and Oxford Economics study found that only half of transformation initiatives succeed. What separates success from failure? Explore real-world data and hear how emerging technologies like AI can help you set goals, gain management buy-in, and identify risks before they derail progress.
Myrna Acosta, Chief Business Officer, SAP Business Transformation Management, SAP
Tuesday, May 20. 2:00 PM - 2:20 PM EDT

BTM1666 | Mastering the art of organizational change management
In SAP software implementations, successful change management is like baking the perfect pie: every ingredient matters. Learn about the essential steps for a smooth transition, from preparing your team to embracing new processes. Hear how to blend communication, training, and leadership into a recipe for lasting implementation success.
Kristen Scheffler, VP Customer Engagement North America, SAP
Wednesday, May 21. 11:30 AM - 12:10 PM EDT

SER2114 | From “A” to “Impact”: Measuring business value with AI
Explore how we use data-driven insights and process mining to create a measurable business impact with our AI scenarios. You can find out how we create a framework to measure and validate value generation and gain actionable insights so that you can improve your business outcomes in an agentic AI world.

I’m excited to have conversations with customers and partners about these topics, both as a representative of SAP Cloud ERP UX Engineering and the ASUG point of contact for user experience and public cloud. Please reach out to me to schedule a time to meet or you can arrange this through your account executive.

Hope to see you there.

entry.title: Side-by-Side Extensibility - Getting started for SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
Dear All,

Are you curious how it feels to “develop” a side-by-side extension for SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 #  SAP Service Cloud Version 2 #with SAP Business Technology Platform .

I published a step-by-step learning 🎓 for the basics. This is not only for developers but for anyone curious! Feel free to try it, share it and teach it!

It is simple and short (in the video I do it in only 16 minutes). You can do it without admin access to a Sales or Service Cloud Version 2 but for the full experience you should have it.

Check out my new beginner friendly SSv2 – Extensibility Workshop which includes:

  • Detailed Step-by-Step written guide to build a web site embedded as a mashup, build a microservice with two cloud functions and connect all those to SSv2
  • The guide comes with a detailed how-to video that shows live every single step on the way (in only 16 minutes)
  • The training does not require any coding skills or knowledge!
  • The training is self-guiding (but having an expert guiding through it helps to fix any upcoming obstacles!)

What you will learn:

What you will learn

In the future I hope I can extend this one with further topics like how to develop custom services + CAP or how to build V2 looking UI’s using web components. 😊 Stay tuned!

Bonus: Are you new to SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2? - check out this video microsite where I show many of the solution capabilities but also some more tech deep dives. All videos are short, hands-on and non-marketing videos 🙂

Cheers

Jens

entry.title: What's New in SAP BTP – Q1 2025
entry.content:
If you tuned in last year (or before), you probably remember our monthly bite-sized innovation highlight videos – SAP BTP Innobytes, where we showcased the coolest new features in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). Well, guess what? We’re back and better than ever in 2025, with even more enthusiasm and a demo-driven approach!

This year, we’ll be bringing you quarterly videos that dive deeper into SAP BTP innovations. While the videos will be a bit longer, they’ll be packed with demos to show these innovations in action and how they can help you achieve more.

To kick things off, we’re starting strong with our first video for Q1! Stay tuned for a closer look at all the exciting things we’ve been working on.

 

App Dev & Automation Highlights

Holistic Views Across Processes

For a holistic view across all processes, we have built dedicated Process Views for Requestors and Process Participants. These views provide information about running instances across multiple processes natively on top of Process or Workflow, without any external configuration.

Process Views has two views dedicated for Requestors and Process Participants named “My Requests” and “My Processes”.

"My Requests” application provides clarity to the Requestors on how their request fits into the workflow, the status of their request, and what actions (if any) they need to take as their request moves through the approval or fulfillment process.

The next application “My Processes” is dedicated to Process Participants like Approvers for example. Process Views help them easily track, understand, and make informed decisions about where their input is needed and how it fits into the larger workflow.

Process views are all about making complex workflows or progress status more digestible. It’s not just about having data; it’s about having the right type of view at the right time to make smarter decisions.

Watch the video demo (starting at 0:40) for more insights!

Learn more:

  • Register for the upcoming ‘What’s New in SAP Build’ webinar: https://sap.to/604200bfg

ABAP Meets SAP Build Code

Imagine seamlessly transitioning from the Cloud into the ABAP world. With SAP Build Code, that’s now a reality! In six easy steps you can integrate an ABAP remote function (RFC) into your Cloud Application Programming (CAP) project:

  1. Setup the CAP Application
  2. Explore Available Functions
  3. Add the ABAP Function to the Project
  4. Add Business Logic
  5. Implement the SAP Fiori UI
  6. Run the Application

Watch the video (starting at 2:16) to see these steps in action!

Learn more:

  • Explore SAP System Functions on the SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/build_code/d0d8f5bfc3d640478854e6f4e7c7584a/892114ce078b4e17a9ff7e751e6330cc.html#explore-sap-system-functions

Joule for Developers with ABAP

Are you looking for an assistant to help you build faster, smoother, and more efficiently? Look no further than Joule! In this demo (starting at 3:46), Sebastian explores how Joule can enhance your experience in ABAP Development Tools, particularly when it comes to efficient unit testing.

With Joule, you can easily identify and mock dependencies, as well as generate unit tests with minimal effort. This makes ABAP Cloud development significantly faster and more efficient, freeing you up to focus on more important tasks.

 Learn more:

  • Joule Speaks ABAP | SAP Community Blog Post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/joule-speaks-abap/ba-p/14018226

Artificial Intelligence Highlight

Reinventing Business Process with Joule and AI Agent Builder

AI agents are changing the game when it comes to business automation. These intelligent agents can plan, make decisions, and execute tasks based on user-defined goals, without the need for predefined processes. In this demo (starting at 5:06), Benjamin dives into how AI agents, powered by SAP’s Agent Builder on SAP BTP and Joule, are helping business users eliminate repetitive tasks and drive efficiency.

Learn more:

  • AI Agents on SAP.com: https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-agents.html

Integration Highlight

New Adapters for Cloud Integration

Today’s enterprises operate in a complex ecosystem of applications and platforms, which makes seamless integration more important than ever. With SAP Integration Suite, we make it easy to connect with over 250 applications, not just within SAP but also with a wide range of third-party systems.

We’re excited to announce the release of four new adapters for our Cloud Integration capability, which will help businesses streamline their integration processes even further. These adapters are now available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

  1. IBM MQ Adapter
  2. Google Pub/Sub Adapter
  3. Azure CosmosDB Adapter
  4. Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter

These new adapters can be easily consumed via the Integration Flow designer. The first time you select an adapter, it’s automatically imported and deployed to your system—making setup both straightforward and efficient. From there, you can quickly connect to your target systems by simply providing the necessary connection details.

Take a closer look at the video (starting at 6:56).

Learn more:

  • SAP integration Suite – ‘Connect Everything’ Blog Series: https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/sap-integration-suite-connect-everything/ba-p/13982527

Data & Analytics Highlight

Knowledge Graph Engine in SAP HANA Cloud

In today’s fast-paced world, making smart decisions requires working with highly connected, diverse, and ever-evolving data sets. SAP HANA Cloud’s Knowledge Graph Engine is here to help you tackle this challenge by improving data querying, generating deeper insights, and boosting the performance of AI agents. Explore how this powerful tool can transform the way you work with complex data in the video demo (starting at 8:02).

Learn more:

  • Connecting the Facts: SAP HANA Cloud’s Knowledge Graph Engine for Business Context SAP Community Blog Post

Cross Platform Highlight

SAP Automation Pilot Catalog for Cloud ALM Ops

The SAP Automation Pilot catalog provides instant access to ready-to-use automation commands, eliminating the need for your teams to manually execute repetitive tasks. These workflows are designed to integrate seamlessly with SAP Cloud ALM, ensuring the right context and commands are applied to the appropriate IT resources in your SAP landscape.

By automating routine operational tasks, SAP Automation Pilot significantly reduces the time spent on manual processes, freeing up valuable resources for more impactful work. Whether you’re managing incidents or performing system checks, SAP Automation Pilot helps take your SAP Cloud ALM operations to the next level.

Take a look at the demo (starting at 9:10).

Learn more:

  • “Building Technical Ops Automation Flows with GenAI (part of the learning journey: Discovering DevOps with SAP BTP )

Share your thoughts 

These videos are for YOU. We would love to hear your impressions. How do you like the video? How can we improve the upcoming videos? Please share your feedback by liking this post or leaving a comment below.

Thank you for your support and happy viewing, sharing, and enjoying!

You Might Also Be Interested In

  • SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos.
  • SAP BTP Talk Podcast: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP.

entry.title: The Rise of Agentic AI: A Deep-Dive in the World of Multi Agent Systems
entry.content:
Introduction

Agents are the talk of the town. As per the predictions of top enterprise companies, Agentic AI is the highest priority in 2025 and forward. Still in early stages of evolution, the capabilities in reasoning and collaboration are breaking records every day. Soon agents are expected to be more independent and handle complete complex business tasks without human intervention.

What does this mean for developers, consultants and architects - A HUGE OPPORTUNITY.

Developing reliable Agents, is important and more critical is to ensure the agents collaborate efficiently to complete the desired tasks.

In my blog, I am going to explain concept of Agentic AI and deep dive into Multi Agent Systems. The intent of this blog is to provide you the starting point and give you a leverage to explore possibilities on your own and ofcourse, with the help of your preferred co-pilot!

Along with the theory, my intention is to showcase a fully functional prototype which you can refer as well - GitHub Repo.

So, buckle up! and let's start 🚀

I will breakdown the article into following parts

  1. Introduction to Agentic AI and Multi Agent Systems
  2. Key components of Agentic AI
  3. Agentic AI flow diagram
  4. Solution Architecture
  5. Code walkthrough
  6. Lessons learnt

Introduction to Agentic AI and Multi Agent System

Without much theory, I will explain my take on:

What is Agentic AI?

  1. AI systems where agents operate independently, taking their decisions and executing tasks without much human intervention.

  2. The agents in Agentic AI framework use tools to enhance the outcomes.

  3. Tools can be APIs, LLMs or documents which can provide data to the agents.

What is Multi Agent System?

  1. Multi Agent System is an implementation and/or extension of Agentic AI.

  2. More than one agents work together on specific tasks.

  3. These agents can also collaborate and delegate the tasks among themselves eventually leading to the final task completion.

Key Components of Agentic AI

In this learning journey, I have chosen autogen as the choice of agentic framework for developing the multi agent system. This doesn't mean that autogen can be used in all agentic scenarios. There are lot of agentic AI frameworks available, each of it having their own pros and cons. I will provide few market leading available frameworks at the end of this blog for you to explore.

Autogen heavily relies on LLM orchestration to collaborate between agents.

Autogen provides a ConversableAgent interface with three types of implementations

  1. AssistantAgent - This agent performs the task
  2. UserProxyAgent - This agent acts as a human proxy and reduces human intervention
  3. GroupChatManager - This agent acts as a manager to manage between agents and delegate the tasks

Below diagram gives a abstracted view of the different personas of agents in Autogen. Reference - https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/0.2/docs/Use-Cases/agent_chat/

Figure 1

Agentic AI Flow Diagram

In this section, I have created 2 simple flow diagrams showcasing how the agents work and interact with each other.

UserProxy and Assistant Agents in Action

The below diagram shows:

  1. The UserProxyAgent delegates the task to AssistantAgent.
  2. The AssistantAgent uses tools which are functions to enrich their outcome based on data.
  3. The AssistantAgent terminates the request once the expected output is achieved and responds to the user proxy agent.
  4. The UserProxyAgent provides the final response to the user.

Figure 2

UserProxy, GroupChatManager and Assistant Agents in Action

In this flow diagram, I have taken a finance use case where we have two AssistantAgents

  1. Finance Agent - Takes care of finance related queries.
  2. CSR Agent - Takes care of customer communication.

The GroupChatManager agent is the orchestrator to get the job done.

The below diagram shows:

  1. The UserProxyAgent delegates the task to GroupChatManager agent.
  2. The GroupChatManager agent delegates the request to either finance AssistantAgent or CSRAssistantAgent or both agents based on the user requests.
  3. The AssistantAgent uses tools which are functions to enrich their outcome based on data.
  4. The GroupChatManager agent terminates the request once the expected output is achieved and responds to the UserProxyAgent.
  5. The UserProxyAgent provides the final response to the user.

Figure 3

 

Solution Architecture

This section outlines a high level solution architecture.

  • The  Agentic AI application is deployed on Cloud Foundry runtime.
  • AI Launchpad  is leveraged to provide a wide set of model library available to choose.
  • The generative-ai-hub-sdk is plugged in to integrate with the underlying model of choice.
  • Backend APIs can be securely integrated leveraging Destination Service.
  • The Agentic AI can be consumed by any Chat Client (Joule for example) of choice as it is being exposed as REST API.

Figure 4

 Code Walkthrough

As you get the high level architecture, now it's time to deep dive into the code. The README file will help you get started. I would like to mention some important files in this section for you to give extra attention when you are browsing through the code:

  1. server.py - The main starting point of your application.
  2. llm_config.py - Central place to store all LLM configurations for all agents.
  3. prompt_config.py - Central place to store all prompts for the agents.
  4. aicoreclient.py - Custom model for LLM if you are not using openai LLM. 
  5. weather_agent.py - Autogen weather agent showcasing UserProxyAgent and AssistantAgent.
  6. finance_group_chat_agent.py - Autogen finance agent showcasing - UserProxyAgent, GroupChatManager and AssistantAgent.

For weather agent, I have integrated with real weather API.

For finance agent, I have mocked the backend calls with sample JSON

I have shared a fragment of log below which shows the agents communicating with each other. Take a look.

user_proxy (to chat_manager):

Can you send a text message to the customer CUST001 as a reminder for his pending invoice INV1005

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
finance_agent (to chat_manager):

***** Suggested function call: extract_customer_id *****
Arguments: 
{"user_input":"Can you send a text message to the customer CUST001 as a reminder for his pending invoice INV1005"}
********************************************************

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

>>>>>>>> EXECUTING FUNCTION extract_customer_id...
user_proxy (to chat_manager):

***** Response from calling function (extract_customer_id) *****
CUST001
****************************************************************

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
finance_agent (to chat_manager):

***** Suggested function call: fetch_customer_details *****
Arguments: 
{"customer_id":"CUST001"}
***********************************************************

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

>>>>>>>> EXECUTING FUNCTION fetch_customer_details...
user_proxy (to chat_manager):

***** Response from calling function (fetch_customer_details) *****
{'customer_id': 'CUST001', 'address': '123 Business St, New York, NY', 'account_manager': 'Michael Johnson', 'account_status': 'Active', 'company': 'Acme Corp', 'email': 'john.doe@acmecorp.com', 'phone': '+1-555-1234-1234'}
*******************************************************************

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
finance_agent (to chat_manager):

I have retrieved the details for customer CUST001. The phone number is +1-555-1234-1234.

Please proceed to send a text message to remind them about the pending invoice INV1005.

Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
csr_agent (to chat_manager):

***** Suggested function call: send_text_message *****
Arguments: 
{"phone_number":"+1-555-1234-1234","message":"Dear Acme Corp team, this is a friendly reminder about your pending invoice INV1005. Please ensure it is paid at your earliest convenience. Thank you."}
******************************************************

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

>>>>>>>> EXECUTING FUNCTION send_text_message...
******Text Message****** : +1-555-1234-1234
###########################################
Message: Dear Acme Corp team, this is a friendly reminder about your pending invoice INV1005. Please ensure it is paid at your earliest convenience. Thank you.

Lessons Learnt

  1. Prompts – Prompts are very important to get the desired output. Write your prompts meticulously. Learn prompt engineering.
  2. Latency – Agents can go in loops at times and increase overall time for response and cost of the request. Agent evaluation is a key.
  3. API calls – Choose your APIs wisely and consider the response times. Reduce multiple API calls.
  4. Heterogenous integrations – APIs are available in different styles - REST, ODATA, SOAP etc. Integrating with different APIs can be a challenge.
  5. Focus – Keep your agents focussed on one particular task. If the task is going to take longer time then better to handle it asynchronously.

Available Agentic AI Frameworks

I have listed current few agentic AI frameworks available and are good starting point to start learning and developing your multi agent systems.

  1. LangGraph - https://www.langchain.com/langgraph 
  2. CrewAI - https://www.crewai.com
  3. Semantic Kernel - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/semantic-kernel/ 

Conclusion

Drawing from my experience, I must emphasize that these insights can serve as a starting point, though their applicability may vary based on specific scenarios. This project will surely give you a holistic view from design and development of Agentic AI solutions and eventually deployment on cloud in production scenarios.

Take a deep breath and start coding  —  GitHub Repo

Stay curious! Keep learning!

Disclaimer: This is not an official reference application or documentation. The thoughts outlined in this blog are based on my experience and learnings about Agentic AI.

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/project/autogen/ 
  2. https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/0.2/docs/tutorial/introduction
  3. https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/0.2/blog/2024/01/26/Custom-Models 
  4. https://github.com/microsoft/autogen/issues/2929

entry.title: So this is it: I’m VERPed!
entry.content:
📢  Today is my last day working for SAP.

Indeed, I've been accepted into the very last round of the Voluntary Early Retirement Plan launched by SAP France in 2024, for which I applied the day of my 62nd birthday, on March 10.

It has been quick, but I'm happy. I thanks my management hierarchy Diana Roesner / André Borchert / Thomas Volmering / Steffen Pietsch / Michael Ameling / Muhammad Alam / Christian Klein (*) for giving me the unique opportunity to stop working 33 months earlier than expected! 😊

(*) yes, I was very low in SAP org chart 😉 but I do not care: what matters to me is to bring my expertise where and when it makes a real impact, and I humbly think it was the case during my 6.5-year tenure in this great company. 

I will really miss my wonderful team of such lovely colleagues in charge of SAP Build Product Management, Enablement and Communications 🥹  ... but I will still be hanging out here on SAP Community and on LinkedIn to follow what they will be doing with SAP Build and broadly SAP Business Technology Platform with Artificial Intelligence and Joule, because I know what will be announced in a few weeks at SAP Sapphire 2025, and for sure they will rock the stage!

 

I would like to heartfully thank Diana Roesner, André Borchert, Thomas Volmering, Michael Ameling, Sebastian Schrötel, Bhagat Nainani, Juergen Mueller and all the colleagues for having supporting me during my 6+ years at SAP, during this amazing journey from SAP Intelligent RPA to SAP Build. 🙏🏻

👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓 I seize this opportunity to promote 4️⃣ learnings on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Build.

Indeed, I won't spoil anything but there will be tremendous announcements in a few weeks: leverage these learnings to be prepared!

✅ An Enterprise Architect’s View on SAP Business Technology Platform

✅ Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build

✅ Compose and Automate with SAP Build the No-Code Way

✅ Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation

📖  Let me also recommend the SAP Build Process Automation book from SAP PRESS!

Order this book now and learn how to create processes, automations, actions, and decisions by following step-by-step instructions with this expert-written guide by my dear colleagues @ArchanaShukla, @Chaitanya_Priya Puvvada, @Stephan Schluchter, Seshadri Sreenivas, and Steven Jacobson.

Finally, I would like to say that I'm very happy because I will spend 2 days in Paris April 14-15, 2 days in Walldorf May 12-13 and finally 2 days in Berlin May 14-15 to hang out with my colleagues and say a warm and grateful goodbye: if you will be there, come say hi! 🥲

And now, for me it's time to…

 

 💙 I wish you all the very best! 💙
